sourceRef.citation: Harsha Sahu, Rekha Pathak, Aman Kumar Tiwari, Rohit Kumar, Ashish  Behra et al. 2026

statement.en: Regenerative Approaches in Veterinary Orthopaedics: Stem Cells and Bioactive Molecules in Bone Tissue Engineering
statement.summary: Bone and cartilage disorders are among the most common causes of lameness, pain, and reduced performance in veterinary patients. Conventional treatments such as fixation, grafting, and pharmacological management often fail to restore complete structure and function, particularly in large bone defects and degenerative joint diseases like osteoarthritis. Bone tissue engineering (BTE) has emerged as a promising regenerative approach that combines stem cells, bioactive molecules, and scaffolds to promote true tissue regeneration rather than simple repair. Mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs), derived from sources such as bone marrow, adipose tissue, dental tissues, and umbilical cord, play a central role due to their regenerative, paracrine, and immunomodulatory properties. proposer: Harsha Sahu, Rekha Pathak, Aman Kumar Tiwari, Rohit Kumar, Ashish  Behra

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a preprint (dated 2026-04-25, future-dated or post-hoc preprint) surveying regenerative veterinary orthopaedics. The field is actively evolving with clinical trials ongoing. Claims about MSC efficacy, scaffold integration, and cartilage regeneration remain partially validated; some mechanisms are established (MSC immunomodulation), others contested (long-term structural integration rates). Status: provisional scientific consensus, not yet settled theorem.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 組織は時間とともに、細胞と分子と足場の共鳴が炎症を制す時に生まれ変わる。
